title: Point add-to-inbox.sh at memory/gtd/inbox.md instead of lists/inbox.md
description: add-to-inbox.sh (tmux/bin/add-to-inbox.sh) is the single shared script behind both the tmux 'bind g' capture and the awesomewm obsidian-inbox.lua prompt. It currently hardcodes inbox_path=$repo/lists/inbox.md. GTD migration (memory/gtd/) is the new canonical location for the 'personal' repo, so this should append to memory/gtd/inbox.md instead.
design: Script is repo-agnostic (repo picked via ~/.config/tmux/task_repo_name, not just 'personal'), so it can't just hardcode memory/gtd/inbox.md — needs to either assume memory/gtd/inbox.md exists for every tracked repo, or fall back to lists/inbox.md when it doesn't (mirrors the design question already open in nvim-8qr for the neovim capture path). Prefer fixing the path directly over symlinking lists/inbox.md -> memory/gtd/inbox.md: it's a one-line change, fixes both tmux and awesomewm captures at once (shared script), and avoids leaving a legacy compatibility shim during the lists/ -> memory/gtd/ migration.
acceptance_criteria: add-to-inbox.sh appends new captures to memory/gtd/inbox.md (or the correct per-repo GTD path) for GTD-migrated repos, falling back sanely for repos without that structure; existing tmux and awesomewm bindings keep working unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented fallback: add-to-inbox.sh now targets <repo>/memory/gtd/inbox.md when that directory exists, falling back to <repo>/lists/inbox.md otherwise. Mirrors nvim-8qr's resolve_inbox_path logic. Verified against the live personal vault and a synthetic non-GTD repo.

title: Make tmux agent keybindings (claude, opencode, pi) dynamically load based on binary detection
description:
tmux/custom-bindings.tmux.conf currently has a static bind for opencode:

  bind o new-window -a -c "#{pane_current_path}" "opencode"

There is no existing bind for claude code or for the pi agent yet. We want all
three agent-launch keybindings to only be registered if the corresponding
binary is actually installed, using the existing if-shell -b pattern already
used elsewhere in this repo (see tmux/common-bindings.tmux.conf and
tmux/options.tmux.conf for the '[ "$(cat ...)" = ... ]' style condition, adapt
to `command -v <bin>` checks instead).

Target bindings:
- claudecode: bind to launch `claude` in a new window, gated on `command -v claude`
- opencode: change key from plain `o` to `C-o` (control-o), gated on `command -v opencode`
- pi: add `C-p` (control-p) to launch the pi agent, gated on `command -v pi` (or whatever the actual pi binary/command name is - confirm before implementing)

Each bind should only take effect if the respective binary is present on
$PATH, so this dotfiles repo stays portable across machines that don't have
all three agents installed.
